diagrams.title: Sliding Window Protocol Operation
diagrams.description: Shows sender and receiver windows with sequence numbers 0-7, illustrating how the window slides as ACKs are received.
elements: Sender window (frames 0-4 sent, awaiting ACKs for 0-1)
ACK packet moving from receiver to sender
Arrow showing window sliding after ACK
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the sliding window advances and manages frame transmission flow

diagrams.title: TCP Flow Control Mechanism
diagrams.description: Depicts TCP sender and receiver with buffer spaces, showing how window size field controls data flow.
elements: Sender buffer with sent unacknowledged data
TCP header with Window Size field highlighted
Zero window scenario when buffer fills
Window probe packets when window is zero
diagrams.purpose: Understand end-to-end flow control in TCP using receiver window advertisements

diagrams.title: Stop-and-Wait vs Continuous Transmission Comparison
diagrams.description: Timeline comparison showing idle time in Stop-and-Wait vs continuous transmission in sliding window.
elements: Stop-and-Wait: Send → Wait ACK → Send → Wait ACK (significant idle time)
Sliding Window: Send 1,2,3,4 → Receive ACKs → Send 5,6,7,8 (minimal idle time)
Frame transmission time and propagation delay labels
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate efficiency improvement of sliding window over Stop-and-Wait protocol
